question_text: Explain why a sharp knife cuts better than a blunt knife.
correct_answer: A sharp knife has a smaller contact area than a blunt knife. When you apply the same force, a smaller area produces higher pressure, which makes it easier to cut through food.
explanation: Pressure depends on both force and area. A sharp blade concentrates the same pushing force onto a much smaller area, creating much higher pressure at the edge, making it cut through materials more easily.
